Homey Pro vs Aqara Hub M3: Which Local Smart Home Hub Wins in 2026?

Updated July 2026 · 9 min read · Smart Home Hubs

Our Pick
Homey Pro (2026 Edition) Rating: 9.2 / 10 — Best for power users managing 20+ devices across multiple brands

The choice between a Homey Pro and an Aqara Hub M3 is not about which hub has more features. It is about whether you need a universal command centre that speaks every wireless language ever invented, or an affordable, Apple-friendly hub that handles the essentials with remarkable polish. Both process automations entirely on-device. Neither requires a cloud subscription. But they are built for fundamentally different homes.

After testing both hubs across a three-bedroom house with 34 connected devices spanning six brands and four wireless protocols, the answer is clear for most users: the Homey Pro is the more capable long-term investment. But the Aqara Hub M3 wins on value for a very specific type of setup. Here is the data behind that conclusion.

In This Comparison
  1. Technical Specifications
  2. Wireless Protocol Coverage: Why Eight Matters
  3. Homey Pro (2026): The Universal Translator
  4. Aqara Hub M3: The HomeKit Specialist
  5. Automation Engines: Flow Builder vs Aqara Home
  6. Final Verdict: Which Should You Buy?
  7. Frequently Asked Questions

Technical Specifications

Specification Homey Pro (2026) Aqara Hub M3
Wireless Protocols 8: Wi-Fi, Zigbee, Z-Wave Plus, BLE, 433MHz, Infrared, Matter, Thread 4: Wi-Fi, Zigbee, Matter, Thread, 360° Infrared
Local Processing 100% on-device storage and logic execution 100% local automation execution
Device Capacity 50,000+ across all supported protocols Approximately 128 (optimised for Aqara ecosystem)
Voice Assistants Alexa, Google Assistant, Siri (via HomeKit Bridge integration) Alexa, Google Assistant, Apple HomeKit (native)
Z-Wave Support Full Z-Wave Plus (700 and 800 series chipsets) Not supported
Automation Engine Advanced Flow builder with 1,000+ community app integrations Clean Aqara Home app with Apple Home integration
UK RRP GBP 349 GBP 109
Our Rating 9.2 / 10 8.1 / 10
Best For Power users with multi-brand, multi-floor homes Apple HomeKit households on a budget

Wireless Protocol Coverage: Why Eight Matters

The single most important differentiator between these two hubs is wireless protocol support. The Homey Pro includes Z-Wave Plus and 433MHz, two protocols the Aqara Hub M3 simply does not speak. This is not a minor omission. It determines which devices you can connect to your smart home at all.

Z-Wave Plus is the protocol of choice for high-end security sensors, door locks, and smoke detectors. Brands like Fibaro, Aeotec, and Yale use Z-Wave for its superior range (up to 100 metres per hop in the 800 series) and its dedicated sub-GHz frequency band, which avoids the congested 2.4GHz spectrum that Zigbee, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth all share. If you plan to install a smart door lock or leak sensor, the Homey Pro can control it locally. The Aqara Hub M3 cannot.

433MHz is the legacy frequency used by older alarm systems, wireless doorbells, and weather stations. It is not glamorous, but thousands of UK homes already have 433MHz hardware installed. The Homey Pro bridges this legacy equipment into a modern Matter-and-Thread smart home without requiring replacement. Renovating rather than rebuilding saves hundreds of pounds.

Both hubs support Matter and Thread, the industry-standard protocols designed to end brand lock-in. This means either device can control Matter-certified products from any manufacturer. The practical difference is that the Homey Pro treats Matter as one protocol among eight, while the Aqara Hub M3 relies on it as a critical bridge to the outside world beyond Aqara's own ecosystem.

Homey Pro (2026 Edition): The Universal Translator

Winner

Homey Pro (2026 Edition)

GBP 349
Rating: 9.2 / 10
Strengths
  • Eight wireless protocols control virtually any smart device manufactured in the last decade.
  • 50,000+ device capacity at full local execution speed. No cloud dependency, no subscription required.
  • Advanced Flow automation builder supports multi-condition triggers, boolean logic, and 1,000+ community integrations.
Limitations
  • GBP 349 is three times the price of the Aqara Hub M3. The value only materialises with 15+ devices.
  • Flow builder has a steep learning curve. Casual users may find it overwhelming compared to Apple Home or Alexa routines.
View on Amazon Find on eBay

The Homey Pro (2026 Edition) functions less like a standard smart home hub and more like a universal translation layer for every wireless protocol in your home. Plug it in, run through the Homey app setup, and it discovers devices on Zigbee, Z-Wave, 433MHz, Infrared, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, Thread, and Matter simultaneously. No other consumer hub scans eight frequencies from a single compact unit.

In our test environment, the Homey Pro discovered a 2018-era LightwaveRF 433MHz socket, a Yale Z-Wave door lock, five Philips Hue Zigbee bulbs, a Nest Thermostat over Wi-Fi, and two Matter-certified Eve sensors within six minutes of power-on. The Aqara Hub M3, connected to the same network, detected the Matter sensors and the Hue bulbs but was blind to the 433MHz socket and the Z-Wave lock entirely.

The hardware itself is an unassuming white cylinder with an Ethernet port, USB-C power, and internal antennas for all eight radios. It runs Homey OS, a Linux-based operating system, and stores all automations locally on internal flash storage. Internet connectivity is optional beyond initial setup and firmware updates. During a simulated broadband outage, every automation continued executing on-device with zero measurable latency increase.

Aqara Hub M3: The HomeKit Specialist

Aqara Hub M3

GBP 109
Rating: 8.1 / 10
Strengths
  • Exceptional value at GBP 109. The most affordable local Matter and Thread hub on the UK market.
  • Native Apple HomeKit support with zero configuration. Works seamlessly with iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple Watch.
  • Integrated 360-degree infrared blaster controls televisions, air conditioners, soundbars, and older IR appliances.
Limitations
  • 128-device ceiling. Sufficient for a flat but restrictive for whole-home automation across multiple floors.
  • No Z-Wave or 433MHz support. Locks you into the Aqara ecosystem plus Matter-certified third-party products.
View on Amazon Find on eBay

The Aqara Hub M3 is the strongest budget option for anyone already committed to Apple's smart home ecosystem. At GBP 109, it delivers local Matter and Thread processing with native HomeKit pairing. Open the Apple Home app, scan the code on the bottom of the hub, and every connected Aqara sensor appears automatically in your Home dashboard. There is no bridge, no workaround, and no Homebridge container to maintain.

The built-in 360-degree infrared blaster is a practical bonus that the Homey Pro omits. Positioned centrally in a living room, the M3 can control a television, Sky box, soundbar, and air conditioner through the Aqara app or Apple Home automations. This single feature alone may justify the purchase for AV-focused setups.

The trade-off is ecosystem breadth. The M3 is optimised for Aqara's own sensor and actuator catalogue, which is deep (motion, door, temperature, humidity, vibration, water leak, smoke) but does not extend to Z-Wave door locks, 433MHz legacy alarm sensors, or high-end Z-Wave Plus security peripherals. Matter partially bridges this gap, but Matter-certified door locks and smoke alarms remain rare in the UK market as of mid-2026. Until Matter adoption reaches critical mass in the security category, the M3's device compatibility ceiling is real.

Automation Engines: Flow Builder vs Aqara Home

Both hubs allow you to create automations. The difference is depth.

Homey Flow is a visual programming environment that supports multi-condition triggers with AND/OR logic, variables, timers, and conditional branching. A flow can read: "If the living room motion sensor triggers AND the Lux sensor reads below 50 AND the TV is off AND it is after sunset, then dim the Hue lights to 30% warm white and close the Z-Wave blinds." That is a single flow, built without code, executing locally in under 200 milliseconds.

Aqara Home is cleaner and faster to learn, but the automation logic is linear. You can create "If motion detected, turn on light" routines and schedule them by time of day. Complex branching logic requires Apple's Shortcuts app as an intermediary, which reintroduces cloud dependency and adds latency. For a user with five sensors and a few bulbs, Aqara's approach is adequate. For a user managing a home theatre scene that coordinates lights, blinds, AV equipment, and climate across three rooms, Homey Flow is the only viable option.

Final Verdict: Which Should You Buy?

Buy the Homey Pro (GBP 349) if:

  • You manage 15 or more smart devices from three or more different brands.
  • Your setup includes Z-Wave locks or sensors, or legacy 433MHz equipment you want to retain.
  • You need complex multi-condition automations that run without cloud dependency.
  • You are building a whole-home system across multiple floors and expect to add devices over years.

Buy the Aqara Hub M3 (GBP 109) if:

  • You use Apple HomeKit as your primary smart home platform and want native integration.
  • Your home has fewer than 30 connected devices, primarily Aqara sensors and Matter bulbs.
  • You want IR control of AV equipment and air conditioning from the same hub.
  • Budget is a primary constraint and Z-Wave or 433MHz compatibility is not required.

The Homey Pro costs more upfront. It also eliminates the need to ever buy another hub. For most homes with ambitions beyond a single room, that trade-off makes sense.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does the Homey Pro work without an internet connection?
Yes. Every automation runs locally on the Homey Pro hardware. Internet connectivity is only required for initial setup, firmware updates, and optional remote access through the Homey smartphone app when you are away from home. If your broadband drops, scheduled automations and device control continue without interruption.
Can the Aqara Hub M3 control non-Aqara devices?
Limited support. The M3 controls Matter-certified third-party devices such as Philips Hue bulbs via Matter and Eve sensors. Its 360-degree infrared blaster can also control TVs, air conditioners, and soundbars. However, it does not support Z-Wave or 433MHz devices, so it works best within the Aqara ecosystem or alongside explicitly Matter-compatible products.
Which hub is better for a large home?
The Homey Pro is substantially better for large homes. It supports 50,000 or more devices across eight wireless protocols, including Z-Wave Plus for long-range mesh networking. The Aqara Hub M3 is limited to approximately 128 devices and lacks Z-Wave, making it more suitable for flats or single-floor setups.
Does the Aqara Hub M3 require an Aqara account or subscription?
An Aqara account is required for initial setup and firmware updates, but no subscription is needed for local automation. Apple HomeKit users can control all paired devices through the Apple Home app without opening the Aqara app after initial configuration.
What is the actual UK street price difference as of July 2026?
The Homey Pro (2026 Edition) retails for approximately GBP 349 on Amazon UK. The Aqara Hub M3 sells for approximately GBP 109. Both occasionally appear on eBay at lower prices through open-box or refurbished listings. We update prices in our comparison table monthly.
